basic_details.job_title: ISRO VSSC Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27: 460 Vacancies, Walk-In Interview 25 July
basic_details.organization_name: Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre (VSSC), ISRO, Department of Space
basic_details.short_information: This section gives a fast overview of the VSSC Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026-27 for candidates who want the key facts at a glance. It covers the organization, total vacancies, selection drive date, and mode of selection in one place.

application_fee.details: The VSSC Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026-27 notification does not mention any application fee, as this is a walk-in selection drive rather than an online application process with fee payment.

age_limit.details: Age relaxation for VSSC Apprentice 2026 varies by category and reservation status, with additional relaxation for PWBD (Persons with Benchmark Disability) candidates. | Category UR OBC SC ST Graduate Apprentice (Engg & Non-Engg) 28 31 33 33 Graduate Apprentice - PWBD 38 41 43 43 Technician Apprentice (Diploma in Engg) 30 33 35 35 Technician Apprentice (Diploma Engg) - PWBD 40 43 45 45 Technician Apprentice (Diploma Commercial Practice) 26 29 31 31 Technician Apprentice (Commercial Practice) - PWBD 36 39 41 41

qualification.details: Eligibility for VSSC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 depends on which category a candidate applies under - Graduate Apprentice or Technician Apprentice - and each has its own minimum marks requirement. Candidates who completed their degree/diploma before April 2022, those awaiting final-year results, or those pursuing M.E/M.Tech are not eligible to apply. | Engineering disciplines (Graduate Apprentice): Engineering degree (four-year, or three-year for lateral entry) from a recognized university with not less than 65% marks or 6.84 CGPA in the respective field. Hotel Management/Catering Technology: First Class degree in Hotel Management/Catering Technology from a recognized university with not less than 60% marks. General Stream (Non-Engineering) Graduates: Bachelor's degree (three-year duration) from a recognized university with not less than 60% marks or 6.32 CGPA. Training will be in administrative areas such as Administration, Finance & Accounts, and Purchase. Technician Apprentice (Engineering Diploma): First Class Diploma in Engineering (three-year duration) from a State Board of Technical Education. Diploma in Commercial Practice: Diploma in Commercial Practice (three-year duration) from a State Board of Technical Education with not less than 6.32 CGPA. | Candidates should also note: where a CGPA-to-percentage conversion formula exists, it is not permitted for eligibility purposes - at least one criterion (either CGPA or percentage as printed on the certificate) must independently meet the norm. Priority will be given to candidates from universities/State Boards of Technical Education under the Southern Region of BoAT (Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, and Puducherry); other candidates will be considered only if there is a shortage of applications in a discipline. | Age relaxation for VSSC Apprentice 2026 varies by category and reservation status, with additional relaxation for PWBD (Persons with Benchmark Disability) candidates. | Only Indian nationals are eligible to apply. Candidates who have already undergone or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act, 1961 and Amendment Act, 1973, or who have one year or more of experience at any institution, are not eligible. Combining apprenticeship across institutions is also not permitted. Candidates who have completed five years from the month and year of passing as specified in the Degree/Diploma certificate are not eligible for apprenticeship training. If a candidate crosses this five-year window while empanelled or waitlisted, they will not be considered for training.

salary_details.details: The VSSC Apprentice stipend differs by category, with Graduate Apprentices earning more per month than Technician Apprentices, in line with the Apprentices Act, 1961 and its amendments. | The duration of training is one year from the date of joining. Canteen and transport facilities are admissible during the training period, but candidates must arrange their own accommodation.

selection_process: The VSSC Apprentice selection process is based on a single walk-in interview/selection drive rather than a written examination. Candidates who clear document verification are ranked on merit for panel formation.

Document Scrutiny: Candidates report to the selection drive venue with original certificates and self-attested copies; VSSC officials scrutinize documents against educational qualifications and other claims. Application Form Submission: Eligible candidates are handed an application form on the spot

to be filled and submitted along with certificate/testimonial copies at the time of the drive. Merit-Based Selection: Selection is based on the highest marks scored by candidates in the relevant qualifying examination
with due weightage to reservation categories. Panel Formation: Induction of apprentices for 2026-27 depends on the candidate's position in the merit panel
subject to availability of vacancies and validity of the panel. NATS 2.0 Registration: Selected candidates are registered under a contract on the NATS 2.0 portal immediately after induction
how_to_apply: There is no VSSC Apprentice apply online facility for this recruitment - applications are accepted only through the in-person walk-in selection drive. Candidates hoping to search for a "VSSC Apprentice apply online 2026" link should instead prepare to attend in person on the notified date.
Visit the VSSC pavilion on 25 July 2026 at Karthika Thirunal Govt V&GHSS for Girls, Manacaud, Thiruvananthapuram, between 9:30 AM and 5:00 PM.
Carry original documents and self-attested copies of all required certificates listed below.
Undergo document scrutiny by VSSC officials at the venue.
Collect and fill the application form provided on the spot after scrutiny.
Submit the duly filled form along with copies of certificates/testimonials to VSSC officials at the time of the selection drive.
Applications will not be accepted through any other means - postal, email, or online submissions are not permitted, and no interim correspondence or canvassing will be entertained.

content_sections.heading: VSSC Apprentice PDF Download Link
content_sections.text: Candidates who want to verify all details directly can download the official VSSC Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026-27 PDF notification, which carries reference number VSSC/R&R/9.2/WII-01/2026 dated 17 July 2026. The notification, along with the OBC-NCL and EWS certificate formats (Annexure 1 and Annexure 2), is displayed through the VSSC website, intranet Ashoka, and newspaper. VSSC Apprentices Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: VSSC Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27 Quick Overview
content_sections.text: This section gives a fast overview of the VSSC Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026-27 for candidates who want the key facts at a glance. It covers the organization, total vacancies, selection drive date, and mode of selection in one place.

rows.details: Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre (VSSC), ISRO, Department of Space
Karthika Thirunal Govt V&GHSS for Girls, Manacaud, Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala
One year from date of joining

content_sections.heading: VSSC Apprentice Important Dates 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates searching for the VSSC Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026 important dates should note that this is a single-day walk-in drive rather than a multi-stage online process. The notification was issued on 17 July 2026, and the selection drive itself takes place just over a week later.

content_sections.heading: VSSC Apprentice Trainee Vacancy 2026: Post-Wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: The VSSC Apprentice vacancy 2026 is split into two categories: Graduate Apprentice (273 posts) for degree holders and Technician Apprentice (187 posts) for diploma holders. Vacancies span engineering disciplines, hotel management, general stream graduates, and commercial practice diploma holders. Candidates should note that the number of positions may change as per the official notification.

content_sections.heading: VSSC Apprentice Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Eligibility for VSSC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 depends on which category a candidate applies under - Graduate Apprentice or Technician Apprentice - and each has its own minimum marks requirement. Candidates who completed their degree/diploma before April 2022, those awaiting final-year results, or those pursuing M.E/M.Tech are not eligible to apply. Engineering disciplines (Graduate Apprentice): Engineering degree (four-year, or three-year for lateral entry) from a recognized university with not less than 65% marks or 6.84 CGPA in the respective field. Hotel Management/Catering Technology: First Class degree in Hotel Management/Catering Technology from a recognized university with not less than 60% marks . General Stream (Non-Engineering) Graduates: Bachelor's degree (three-year duration) from a recognized university with not less than 60% marks or 6.32 CGPA . Training will be in administrative areas such as Administration, Finance & Accounts, and Purchase. Technician Apprentice (Engineering Diploma): First Class Diploma in Engineering (three-year duration) from a State Board of Technical Education. Diploma in Commercial Practice: Diploma in Commercial Practice (three-year duration) from a State Board of Technical Education with not less than 6.32 CGPA . Candidates should also note: where a CGPA-to-percentage conversion formula exists, it is not permitted for eligibility purposes - at least one criterion (either CGPA or percentage as printed on the certificate) must independently meet the norm. Priority will be given to candidates from universities/State Boards of Technical Education under the Southern Region of BoAT (Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, and Puducherry); other candidates will be considered only if there is a shortage of applications in a discipline. Age relaxation for VSSC Apprentice 2026 varies by category and reservation status, with additional relaxation for PWBD (Persons with Benchmark Disability) candidates. Only Indian nationals are eligible to apply. Candidates who have already undergone or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act, 1961 and Amendment Act, 1973, or who have one year or more of experience at any institution, are not eligible . Combining apprenticeship across institutions is also not permitted. Candidates who have completed five years from the month and year of passing as specified in the Degree/Diploma certificate are not eligible for apprenticeship training. If a candidate crosses this five-year window while empanelled or waitlisted, they will not be considered for training.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The VSSC Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026-27 notification does not mention any application fee, as this is a walk-in selection drive rather than an online application process with fee payment.

content_sections.heading: VSSC Apprentice Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: The VSSC Apprentice selection process is based on a single walk-in interview/selection drive rather than a written examination. Candidates who clear document verification are ranked on merit for panel formation. Document Scrutiny: Candidates report to the selection drive venue with original certificates and self-attested copies; VSSC officials scrutinize documents against educational qualifications and other claims. Application Form Submission: Eligible candidates are handed an application form on the spot, to be filled and submitted along with certificate/testimonial copies at the time of the drive. Merit-Based Selection: Selection is based on the highest marks scored by candidates in the relevant qualifying examination, with due weightage to reservation categories. Panel Formation: Induction of apprentices for 2026-27 depends on the candidate's position in the merit panel, subject to availability of vacancies and validity of the panel. NATS 2.0 Registration: Selected candidates are registered under a contract on the NATS 2.0 portal immediately after induction, via www.nats.education.gov.in.

content_sections.heading: VSSC Apprentice Apply Online / How to Apply 2026
content_sections.text: There is no VSSC Apprentice apply online facility for this recruitment - applications are accepted only through the in-person walk-in selection drive. Candidates hoping to search for a "VSSC Apprentice apply online 2026" link should instead prepare to attend in person on the notified date. Visit the VSSC pavilion on 25 July 2026 at Karthika Thirunal Govt V&GHSS for Girls, Manacaud, Thiruvananthapuram, between 9:30 AM and 5:00 PM. Carry original documents and self-attested copies of all required certificates listed below. Undergo document scrutiny by VSSC officials at the venue. Collect and fill the application form provided on the spot after scrutiny. Submit the duly filled form along with copies of certificates/testimonials to VSSC officials at the time of the selection drive. Applications will not be accepted through any other means - postal, email, or online submissions are not permitted, and no interim correspondence or canvassing will be entertained.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for VSSC Apprentice Selection Drive
content_sections.text: Candidates attending the VSSC Apprentice walk-in interview must carry a specific set of original and self-attested documents, along with category-specific certificates where applicable. B.E/B.Tech/Bachelor in Hotel Management/Non-Engineering Graduation/Diploma Certificate (Provisional/Final) B.E/B.Tech/Bachelor in Hotel Management/Non-Engineering Graduation/Diploma Consolidated Mark list (Provisional/Final) SSLC/SSC/AISSE/ICSE certificate as proof of date of birth Experience certificate (if any) and one passport-size photograph SC/ST candidates: Copy of valid Community/Caste Certificate issued by Tahsildar (Taluk Office) OBC candidates: Copy of valid Non-Creamy Layer Certificate issued by Tahsildar in the prescribed Government of India format EWS candidates: Copy of valid Income & Asset Certificate issued by Tahsildar for financial year 2026-27 PWBD candidates: Certificate of Disability issued by competent authority

content_sections.heading: VSSC Apprentice Stipend / Salary 2026
content_sections.text: The VSSC Apprentice stipend differs by category, with Graduate Apprentices earning more per month than Technician Apprentices, in line with the Apprentices Act, 1961 and its amendments. The duration of training is one year from the date of joining. Canteen and transport facilities are admissible during the training period, but candidates must arrange their own accommodation.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid While Applying for VSSC Apprentice 2026
content_sections.text: Many candidates lose out on apprenticeship opportunities due to avoidable errors rather than lack of eligibility. Being aware of these common pitfalls can improve a candidate's chances at the selection drive. Carrying photocopies instead of originals: The notification clearly requires original documents plus self-attested copies - candidates who bring only photocopies risk rejection at the scrutiny stage. Applying with a CGPA-converted percentage: Since conversion of CGPA to percentage is not accepted for eligibility, candidates should check their actual certificate figures rather than calculating an equivalent percentage themselves. Ignoring the five-year passing window: Candidates close to or past five years since their qualification's passing month/year should double-check eligibility before traveling to the venue. Missing category certificates: OBC candidates in particular should ensure their Non-Creamy Layer certificate is current and in the prescribed Government of India format - an outdated or wrongly formatted certificate can lead to disqualification. Assuming an online application exists: Since this is a walk-in-only drive, candidates searching for an online portal may miss the actual selection date entirely.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in VSSC Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27?
faq.answer: There are 460 total vacancies - 273 Graduate Apprentice posts and 187 Technician Apprentice posts across various engineering and non-engineering disciplines.

faq.question: Q2. What is the VSSC Apprentice selection drive date?
faq.answer: The selection drive is on 25 July 2026 (Saturday) from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM at Karthika Thirunal Govt V&GHSS for Girls, Manacaud, Thiruvananthapuram.

faq.question: Q3. Is there an online application for VSSC Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: No, there is no online application. Applications are accepted only in person at the walk-in selection drive; applications through any other means will not be accepted.

faq.question: Q4. What is the stipend for VSSC Graduate and Technician Apprentices?
faq.answer: Graduate Apprentices receive ₹12,300 per month, and Technician Apprentices receive ₹10,900 per month, for a one-year training period.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for VSSC Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: As on 31 July 2026, the age limit for Graduate Apprentice (UR) is 28 years, and for Technician Apprentice it is 30 years (Engineering Diploma) or 26 years (Commercial Practice Diploma), with relaxation for OBC, SC, ST, and PWBD candidates.

faq.question: Q6. What documents are required for the VSSC Apprentice selection drive?
faq.answer: Candidates need original degree/diploma certificates, consolidated mark lists, SSLC/SSC/AISSE/ICSE certificate as date-of-birth proof, experience certificate (if any), a passport photo, and applicable category certificates (SC/ST, OBC-NCL, EWS, or PWBD).

faq.question: Q7. Who is eligible for VSSC Graduate Apprentice posts?
faq.answer: Candidates with an engineering degree with at least 65% marks/6.84 CGPA, a Hotel Management degree with 60% marks, or a general stream bachelor's degree with 60% marks/6.32 CGPA are eligible, provided they meet the other conditions.

faq.question: Q8. Can candidates who already completed an apprenticeship apply again?
faq.answer: No, candidates who have already undergone or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act, or who have one year or more of institutional experience, are not eligible to apply.

faq.question: Q9. Does completing VSSC Apprentice training guarantee a permanent job?
faq.answer: No, the notification specifies that mere completion of one year of apprenticeship training at VSSC or any ISRO/Department of Space unit does not confer any right to permanent employment.

faq.question: Q10. What happens after selection in the VSSC Apprentice drive?
faq.answer: Selected candidates are registered under a contract on the NATS 2.0 portal immediately after induction, through www.nats.education.gov.in, and induction is based on merit panel position, vacancy availability, and panel validity.
